title: Valerian tincture for mild insomnia
subtitle: A 40% spirit tincture of valerian root, the strongest traditional sleep preparation
excerpt: Valerian root macerated in 40% vodka for four to six weeks, then taken by the drop before bed. The tincture concentrates valerian's sedative and sleep-onset compounds more effectively than any hot infusion. The most documented traditional sleep herb in the western herbal pharmacopoeia.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve, A Modern Herbal (1931), valerian entry, sedative and nervine action, tincture as the preferred preparation. King's American Dispensatory (Felter and Lloyd, 1898), valerian tincture for nervous insomnia. EMA HMPC monograph on Valeriana officinalis radix (2016), confirms traditional use for mild nervous tension and sleep disturbance; recommends dry extract or tincture as the effective preparation.

content.text: Valerian tincture is a strong kitchen sleep preparation. Dried valerian root soaked in 40% vodka for four to six weeks, then strained into a dropper bottle. A kitchen tradition long made for mild restless nights and trouble falling asleep. The alcohol draws out the earthy sleep compounds in the root better than a hot tea ever does. About 30 minutes of work spread across six weeks; the finished bottle keeps for two years.

attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Sedatives and sleep medication: valerian potentiates the effects of barbiturates, benzodiazepines, and other sedative drugs. Do not combine valerian tincture with prescription sleep medication without discussing with your prescriber.

Driving: valerian causes drowsiness in some people at therapeutic doses. Do not drive or operate machinery until you know how the tincture affects you personally. Take it only in the evening.

Alcohol content: each 5 ml dose of a 1:5 tincture made with 40% spirit contains approximately 2 ml of vodka, roughly 40% of a standard unit. Negligible for most adults at the suggested dose, but note this if you are alcohol-sensitive or avoiding alcohol for religious or medical reasons.

Pregnancy: valerian is not recommended in pregnancy. Consult a herbalist.

Children: valerian tincture is not appropriate for children under twelve without herbalist supervision.

Course length: valerian is safe for several weeks of consistent use; some authorities suggest a two-week break after every four to six weeks of daily use. It is not a long-term substitute for addressing the cause of persistent insomnia.

See your doctor for insomnia lasting more than four weeks, insomnia with depression or anxiety, suspected sleep apnoea, or daytime symptoms that are impairing work and relationships.
attrs.title: Important safety notes

items.name: Dried valerian root
items.prepNote: cut and sifted dried root, the root gives this tincture its characteristic earthy, musky smell

content.text: This is a 1:5 tincture, 1 part herb to 5 parts vodka by weight. That ratio is the standard for a valerian root tincture in both old herbals and modern reviews.

Place {{dried-valerian-root}} of dried valerian root in a wide-mouth jar. Pour {{vodka-40-percent}} of vodka over the root, the liquid should cover the herb completely with at least 1 cm of spirit above the top of the root. Seal the jar tightly. Label with the date and the contents. The valerian will smell immediately: earthy, musky, and slightly unpleasant, this is expected and does not mean anything is wrong.

Store the sealed jar in a cool, dark cupboard. Shake or turn the jar daily for the first two weeks to keep the herb moving through the liquid. After four weeks the tincture is usable; six weeks gives a fuller extraction. The liquid will be dark amber-brown.

Line a sieve with muslin cloth over a jug. Pour the entire contents of the jar through the muslin. Gather the muslin around the spent root and squeeze firmly to extract all remaining liquid. Decant into amber dropper bottles using the small funnel. Label with contents, date, and dose instructions.

Adults: 2, 4 ml (40, 80 drops, or roughly half to one full dropper) taken in a small amount of water 30 minutes to one hour before bed. Start at the lower dose (2 ml) for the first three nights to see how it suits you, then increase to 4 ml if needed. That is the standard traditional dose range for valerian root tincture. The traditional course is two to four weeks; take a break after six weeks of regular use.
Finished tincture keeps 2 years in a sealed amber bottle at room temperature away from light. The alcohol acts as a preservative.
When NOT to use this remedy

Do not combine with prescription sedatives or sleep medication. Do not use in pregnancy. Do not take before driving. See your doctor for insomnia lasting more than four weeks, insomnia with depression or severe anxiety, or daytime functioning that is significantly impaired.

recipe.yieldDescription: about 200 ml (100, 200 doses at standard dosing)
